patch.value: pm history-repair --all: bulk drift repair across streams (GH-171)
history-repair takes a single id; add --all (and pm health fix_hint) to repair every drifted stream in one audited pass. GitHub issue #171.

value.author: history-health-agent
value.text: Implemented pm history-repair --all. Design: (1) target contract enforced by shared assertHistoryRepairTarget (exactly one of <id> | --all; clear usage errors) used by BOTH the commander action and the MCP pm_run history-repair case (schema contract: id+all optional, anyOfRequired [[id],[all]]). (2) Bulk pass reuses scanHistoryDrift (the exact pm health scan) then runs the existing audited single-stream runHistoryRepair per drifted stream, so ownership check, lock acquisition, post-repair no-drift verification, --message audit marker, and per-stream --force all apply unchanged. (3) Failure isolation: each stream repair is try/caught into a per-stream row (repaired | skipped_clean | failed + error string); one bad stream never aborts the pass; the CLI sets exit code 1 only when totals.failed > 0. (4) Output: {all, dry_run, scanned_streams, drifted_streams, streams[] (drifted rows only — clean streams stay row-free for token economy), totals, warnings}. (5) pm health remediation: registry summaries mention --all, and health.ts rewrites the history_drift remediation_map commands to 'pm history-repair --all' when counts.drifted > 1.

patch.value: history-repair only accepted a single <id>; multi-stream drift required one manual command per stream and pm health could only suggest the per-item template.

pm history-repair --all scans with the same drift scan pm health uses and runs the audited single-stream repair (ownership, lock, no-drift verify, --message marker, --force) per drifted stream; one compact row per drifted stream (repaired/skipped_clean/failed) plus totals; <id> and --all mutually exclusive with clear usage errors on both CLI and MCP; exit 1 only when a stream failed; history_drift remediation_map rewrites to 'pm history-repair --all' when >1 stream is drifted. Wired through contracts, MCP schema, completions (bash/zsh/fish), docs, and tests.

Shipped pm history-repair --all (GH-171): bulk audited drift repair across all streams with per-stream result rows, failure isolation, and exit-nonzero-only-on-failure; health remediation now points multi-stream drift at the bulk pass.
